human_use.rationale: The Artifact is a human-operated mobile checkout. Its success depends on discoverability, navigation, input burden, error recovery, feedback, accessibility, touch ergonomics, interruption recovery, perceived latency, destructive-action safety, and cognitive load across cross-disability use.

lens_findings.finding: The labelled sections and persistent review make required work apparent, although the full single-page length delays the lower sections.
Skip navigation, section edit controls, and reverse traversal worked; the compacted sections remained recoverable.
All inputs are reachable in one flow with autocomplete-oriented field types, but address and payment remain a long sequential form.
An empty explicit submission reached the error state and the completed correction path retained values.
Completion, restored-progress, and single-confirmation messages were observed, but status is less directive before the user reaches review.
First Tab exposed a skip link; compacted-body traversal found no hidden focus target; no console/runtime errors were recorded.
All three operated widths had scrollWidth equal to clientWidth; controls retain visible spacing, though the long page increases scrolling.
Reload restored Alex Morgan while payment fields were empty.
Reduced motion reported no duration over 10 ms; repeated activation gave exactly one confirmation, with a clear local completion status.
Review edit and duplicate-submit behavior were safe, but no clear-progress confirmation affordance was discoverable to test.
Optional compact summaries, section completion state, and persistent review reduce memory load without concealing completed details.

strengths: Optional compact summaries reduce revisiting completed work without removing it from keyboard traversal.
Reload restored contact and withheld payment; a repeated Place order produced one confirmation.
defects: No discoverable clear-progress confirmation control was available during the operated flow.
The compacted review remains long on a narrow screen.
uncertainty: The accepted objective report establishes the technical gates; this qualitative run did not infer any identity or rerun them.
synthesis_adoption_rejection_recommendat: Adopt the optional compact-completed pattern only: it preserved keyboard exclusion from hidden bodies (supplemental hidden-focus count 0). Reject copying the absence of a discoverable clear-progress confirmation affordance.
objective_reports: Accepted as pass; not rerun or overridden.
